content: Intrinsic curiosity signals from ICM/RND curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be repurposed as a natural uncertainty metric for uncertainty-based memory sample selection in deep active agents, where prediction error from curiosity modules directly quantifies how much an agent still has to learn from a stored memory sample. Unlike hand-designed uncertainty heuristics, this approach ties memory selection directly to the agent's intrinsic exploration drive, prioritizing samples that align with what the agent is already motivated to learn.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
